title: Inside the foundation pour — a week the ground remembers
excerpt: A quiet account of the artisans who led the pour, and what it means for the house taking shape above it. The concrete was mixed before dawn; by midday the mould was set.

title: The families whose lives are already changing
excerpt: Voices from the village on what the house means before its doors have opened.

title: Why Wasimi? The word that started it all
excerpt: Tracing the name back to its roots, and the prayer that named the place.

title: Water access solved: the borehole story
excerpt: How one infrastructure problem, solved quietly, changed the daily rhythm of the site.

title: The engineers who gave up their Saturdays
excerpt: Five professionals, one site, and a dozen weekends they will not forget.

title: A conversation with the Baale of Wasimi
excerpt: On land, legacy, and what this house means for the village.
